title: Argparse Interface, Help, Options, and Usage Errors · Runtime Lens: Parser Testability
summary: The parser abbreviates ambiguous flags, hides defaults and units, accepts negative limits, prints colored help into snapshots, and couples parse_args directly to analysis. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the parser testability increment in testable user-facing command interface. Learners progress through pre…
objectives: Parse supplied argument lists separately from sys.argv and return a configuration record for testing.
Write task-oriented usage, descriptions, metavariables, defaults, units, examples, exit behavior, and accessibility notes.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-argparse-interface-2
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-argparse-interface-2.
# Case 7417: an assistive-technology terminal review.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts.
def worked_bookbot_argparse_interface_2(arguments: int = 6, invalid_arguments: int = 3, invalid_rejected: int = 3, help_has_units: bool = True, abbreviations_disabled: bool = True, color_controlled: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "corpusworked2",
        "policy2dekrow",
        "revision7417",
        "commandworked27417",
        "report2dekrow7417",
        "test7417worked2",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if arguments < 1 or invalid_arguments < 0:
        return False, arguments, "argument-shape"
    if invalid_rejected != invalid_arguments:
        return False, invalid_rejected, "argument-validation"
    if not help_has_units:
        return False, arguments, "help-contract"
    if not abbreviations_disabled or not color_controlled:
        return False, arguments, "parser-reproducibility"
    assert invalid_rejected == invalid_arguments
    return True, arguments, "testable-cli"

# Evidence: bookbot-parser-testability-17yfry
# Competency: bookbot-parser-testability.
# Case 5619: a clean-environment package release.
# Operating constraint: keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of host file, process, Git, package-install, credential, and release effects.
# Changed case: install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases.
def evidence_bookbot_parser_testability_17yfry(arguments: int = 6, invalid_arguments: int = 3, invalid_rejected: int = 3, help_has_units: bool = True, abbreviations_disabled: bool = True, color_controlled: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_17yfry = "17yfry-yrfy71-5619"
    assert evidence_variant_17yfry
    evidence_axes_17yfry = (
        "corpus17yfry",
        "policyyrfy71",
        "revision5619",
        "command17yfry5619",
        "reportyrfy715619",
        "test561917yfry",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_17yfry)) == 6
    if arguments < 1 or invalid_arguments < 0:
        return False, arguments, "argument-shape"
    if invalid_rejected != invalid_arguments:
        return False, invalid_rejected, "argument-validation"
    if not help_has_units:
        return False, arguments, "help-contract"
    if not abbreviations_disabled or not color_controlled:
        return False, arguments, "parser-reproducibility"
    assert invalid_rejected == invalid_arguments
    return True, arguments, "testable-cli"

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Parse supplied argument lists separately from sys.argv and return a configuration record for testing.

At Bookbot and Python Defend the Parser Testability model · Theory Parser Testability, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory Bookbot team handles case 8621 during an offline public-library reading report. Build deterministic Python evidence to define and validate a reproducible task-oriented argparse interface with useful help; keep stdout machine-safe, diagnostics on stderr, status explicit, and every signal understandable without color; then change LF to CRLF, remove the final newline, and preserve the declared word and line policy. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Parse supplied argument lists separately from sys.argv and return a configuration record for testing.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Parser Testability model · Theory Parser Testability, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Testing a parser requires launching a subprocess for every option.
